title: galium multiflorum subsp. hirsutum
intro: Galium multiflorum subsp. hirsutum, also known as beaked bedstraw or beaked bedstraw subspecies, is a flowering plant in the bedstraw family (Rubiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Galium multiflorum subsp. hirsutum include relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations, making it a valuable remedy for women's health issues. Herbal preparations of Galium multiflorum subsp. hirsutum can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties and reap its benefits.
